x-enumeration-literals.name: Sales Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sales invoice is a billing document issued by a seller to a buyer requesting payment for goods or services that have been delivered.

Comment:
Example:
A computer retailer sells 10 laptops to a company and issues a sales invoice for €12,000 payable within 30 days.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Purchase Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A purchase invoice is an invoice received by a buyer from a supplier requesting payment for goods or services purchased.

Comment:
Example:
A restaurant buys food supplies from a wholesaler and receives a purchase invoice for €2,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Proforma Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A proforma invoice is a preliminary invoice sent before the actual delivery of goods or services to provide cost details or request advance payment.

Comment:
Example:
A furniture exporter sends a proforma invoice for €15,000 to a buyer before shipping the goods.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A credit invoice is issued to reduce or cancel a previously issued invoice due to returns, discounts, or billing errors.

Comment:
Example:
A customer returns defective products worth €500, so the seller issues a credit invoice for €500.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Debit Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A debit invoice is issued to increase the amount payable due to additional charges or correction of an underbilled invoice.

Comment:
Example:
A logistics company initially billed €800, but additional delivery costs of €150 occur, so a debit invoice for €150 is issued.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Recurring Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A recurring invoice is automatically generated at regular intervals for ongoing services or subscriptions.

Comment:
Example:
A software company sends a monthly subscription invoice of €50 to its customers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interim Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An interim invoice is issued during the progress of a long-term project to request partial payment before completion.

Comment:
Example:
A construction company sends an interim invoice of €20,000 after completing the first stage of building work.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Final Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A final invoice is the last invoice issued after a project or service is completed, including the remaining balance.

Comment:
Example:
After finishing a renovation project costing €60,000, the contractor sends a final invoice for the remaining €10,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Commercial Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A commercial invoice is used in international trade to declare the value, quantity, and description of goods for customs clearance.

Comment:
Example:
An exporter sends a commercial invoice for €25,000 worth of electronics to a buyer in another country.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Self-Billing Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A self-billing invoice is created by the buyer on behalf of the supplier under an agreed billing arrangement.

Comment:
Example:
A supermarket generates invoices for farmers who supply fresh vegetables weekly.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Timesheet Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A timesheet invoice is based on the number of hours worked and is commonly used in consulting or freelance services.

Comment:
Example:
A consultant charges €100 per hour for 40 hours, issuing a timesheet invoice for €4,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mixed Invoice
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A mixed invoice contains both debit and credit adjustments within the same invoice document.

Comment:
Example:
An invoice includes €2,000 for new products but deducts €300 for returned items, resulting in a net invoice of €1,700.
